pattern: Taking the per-letter minimum frequency across all words leaves exactly the characters common to every word, duplicates included.
hints: A character belongs in the answer as many times as the word that contains it the fewest times.
Count letters in each word, then for every letter keep the smallest count seen across all words.
Start from the first word's letter counts and intersect with each subsequent word by lowering each count to the minimum, then emit each letter that many times.

edge_inputs.description: no shared character yields empty result
single word returns all its characters
one word missing a letter drops it entirely
common letter limited by the scarcest word

stress.description: several short words over a tiny alphabet so overlaps are frequent
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 30))
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    words = []
    for _ in range(m):
        length = rng.randint(1, 8)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(length)))
    return [words]
stress.note: small alphabet maximises common-letter cases including empties
